questions.question: How long have you had this fever?
How high is the temperature?
Do you have chills or shivering?
Are you experiencing body aches?

advice: Rest, stay hydrated. See a doctor if fever lasts more than 3 days or exceeds 103°F.

questions.question: How long have you had these symptoms?
Do you have a sore throat?
Are you experiencing difficulty breathing?
Do you have a fever along with the cough?

advice: Rest, drink warm fluids, use steam inhalation. See a doctor if breathing becomes difficult.

questions.question: Where is the headache located?
How severe is the pain (1–10)?
Is the headache accompanied by nausea or vomiting?
Does light or noise worsen the headache?

advice: Rest in a dark, quiet room. Drink water. Seek urgent care if headache is sudden, severe, or the worst of your life.

questions.question: Where exactly is the pain?
Do you have nausea or vomiting?
Do you have diarrhea or loose stools?
When did the pain start?

advice: Stay hydrated with ORS. Avoid solid food initially. See a doctor if pain is severe or blood appears in stools.

questions.question: How does the chest pain feel?
Does the pain spread to arm, jaw, or shoulder?
Are you short of breath?
Do you sweat or feel dizzy along with the pain?

advice: Chest pain can be serious. Call emergency services (112) immediately if pain is severe or spreading.

questions.question: Where is the back pain located?
Did the pain start after lifting or injury?
Does the pain radiate to legs?
How long have you had this pain?

advice: Rest, apply warm compress. See a physiotherapist. Seek immediate care if you have leg weakness or bladder issues.

questions.question: Where is the rash located?
Is the rash itchy?
Did the rash appear after eating something or touching a substance?
Is there swelling around the rash or on face/lips?

advice: Avoid known allergens. Antihistamines may help. Seek emergency care if swelling affects face/throat or breathing.

questions.question: What symptoms do you have in the eye?
Is one eye or both eyes affected?
Is there sudden vision loss?
Did you have contact with someone with eye infection?

advice: Do not rub eyes. Keep eyes clean. See an eye doctor if vision is affected or pain is severe.

questions.question: Which joint(s) are affected?
Is there swelling or redness in the joint?
Is the pain worse in the morning?
Did the pain start after an injury?

advice: Rest the joint, apply ice/warm compress. See a doctor for evaluation of arthritis or injury.

questions.question: How severe is the breathing difficulty?
Do you hear wheezing when you breathe?
Do you have a history of asthma?
Did the breathing difficulty start suddenly?

advice: Breathing difficulty is serious. If severe, call 112 immediately. Sit upright and stay calm.

questions.question: Are you experiencing excessive thirst?
Do you urinate frequently?
Are you feeling unusually tired or weak?
Do you have a known diagnosis of diabetes?

advice: Monitor blood sugar levels. Follow prescribed diet. Consult your doctor for medication adjustment.

questions.question: What are your current BP readings?
Are you on BP medication?
Do you have headache or dizziness?
Are you experiencing chest pain or palpitations?

advice: Check BP readings regularly. Take medication as prescribed. See a cardiologist if readings are extreme.

questions.question: How old is the child?
What is the child's temperature?
Is the child eating and drinking normally?
Is the child unusually sleepy or irritable?

advice: Keep child hydrated. Use fever-reducing medicine as age-appropriate. See a pediatrician if infant under 3 months has fever.

questions.question: What symptoms are you experiencing?
How severe is the pain?
Is the pain accompanied by nausea or vomiting?
Is there any unusual discharge?

advice: Use pain relievers as needed. Consult a gynecologist for diagnosis of hormonal or structural issues.

questions.question: Which ear is affected?
Is there any discharge from the ear?
Is there any hearing loss?
Did symptoms start after swimming or water exposure?

advice: Do not insert anything in the ear. See an ENT specialist for ear pain with discharge or hearing loss.

questions.question: Do you have burning or pain while urinating?
Is there increased frequency of urination?
Do you see blood in the urine?
Do you have fever or back/flank pain?

advice: Drink plenty of water. See a doctor for urine test and appropriate antibiotic treatment.

questions.question: What are your main symptoms?
How long have you been experiencing these feelings?
Is it affecting your daily life?
Have you had similar episodes before?

advice: Talk to a trusted person. Practice breathing exercises. Consult a doctor or counselor — seeking help is strength.

questions.question: How many times have you vomited in the last 24 hours?
Is there blood in the vomit?
Do you have abdominal pain along with vomiting?
Can you keep fluids down?

advice: Stay hydrated with small sips of ORS. Avoid solid food temporarily. See a doctor if vomiting persists or blood is present.

questions.question: How long have you been feeling weak?
Is the weakness accompanied by fever?
Are you eating and drinking normally?
Any recent illness or heavy physical work?

advice: Rest, eat balanced meals, stay hydrated. See a doctor if weakness is sudden, severe, or accompanied by other serious symptoms.

questions.question: How many loose stools in the last 24 hours?
Is there blood or mucus in the stool?
Do you have stomach cramps?
Are you able to drink fluids?

advice: Use ORS to prevent dehydration. Eat plain foods (rice, banana). See a doctor if blood in stool, high fever, or severe dehydration.
